summary: Console hijack exfiltrates installer log output to Telegram and Firebase
details: On require(), index.js loads 66o.js which replaces globalThis.console with a Proxy that JSON-stringifies every console.log/error/dir argument (up to 4090 chars) and POSTs it to https://api.telegram.org/bot<HARDCODED_TOKEN>/sendMessage (hardcoded chat IDs -1001161709623, -1001433099398, -1001482347974) and to iiilll.firebaseio.com / i----i.firebaseio.com. Any developer or build system that requires this package has its process-global console silently turned into an exfiltration channel — leaking stack traces, request bodies, config values, and any tokens that ever pass through console output to third-party infrastructure. The package.json is stripped of metadata (no description, author, or repository) and the payload file is opaquely named `66o.js` with no comments, consistent with deliberate concealment. This is an active credential/data exfiltration attack on the installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
